asset_details.name: Private Key
asset_details.description: Cryptographic key used to access and control assets in a wallet.

behavior_details.name: Burn HONEY
behavior_details.description: External User burns $HONEY to redeem collateral assets on Honey Swap.

data_flow_details.description: User sends collateral assets to Honey Swap to mint $HONEY.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: Claim proof validation logic is flawed, allowing altered or forged proofs.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to alter the outcome of an incentive claim.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful tampering could lead to theft of incentives or double-claiming.

assumptions: The Incentive Distributor Contract (ID 8) does not strictly or correctly validate claim proofs.
An attacker can generate valid-looking claim proofs for entitlements they don't own.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust on-chain validation for claim proofs and associate proofs with unique, non-reusable entitlements.

description: An attacker could tamper with the transaction or forge the claim proof used in the 'Claim Incentives' behavior, allowing them to claim incentives belonging to other users or claim more than entitled.

component_details.name: User Wallet
component_details.description: Software or hardware storing user private keys and interacting with the blockchain.

behavior_details.name: Stake Assets
behavior_details.description: External User stakes PoL-eligible assets in a Reward Vault.

data_flow_details.description: Honey Swap sends minted $HONEY or vault shares to the user wallet.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract or network infrastructure is susceptible to transaction spam.

actor_risk.description: An external user capable of submitting a high volume of transactions.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Could prevent legitimate users from casting votes during critical governance periods.

assumptions: Transaction fees for voting are low enough to make spamming economically viable.
The Governance Contract (ID 5) or underlying network infrastructure cannot handle a large volume of concurrent voting transactions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement dynamic transaction fees, optimize contract processing for voting, or introduce rate limiting mechanisms.

description: An attacker could flood the Governance Contract with vote transactions, consuming network resources and preventing legitimate users from submitting their votes or proposals within the required timeframe.

component_details.name: User Wallet
component_details.description: Software or hardware storing user private keys and interacting with the blockchain.

behavior_details.name: Stake Assets
behavior_details.description: External User stakes PoL-eligible assets in a Reward Vault.

data_flow_details.description: Honey Swap sends minted $HONEY or vault shares to the user wallet.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract fails to correctly verify the asserted voting power against actual BGT holdings/delegations.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to vote with more power than they possess, gaining undue influence over governance outcomes.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Allows attacker to skew governance decisions and potentially pass malicious proposals.

assumptions: The Governance Contract (ID 5) contains a bug that allows a user to claim and exercise voting power exceeding their actual BGT balance or delegated amount.

mitigation.strategy: Thoroughly audit and test the governance contract's voting power calculation and validation logic.

description: An attacker could exploit a vulnerability in the Governance Contract (ID 5) to cast votes with fraudulently inflated voting power, effectively achieving an elevation of privilege within the governance system.

asset_details.name: Private Key
asset_details.description: Cryptographic key used to access and control assets in a wallet.

attack_surface.data_description: Claim transaction processing and state updates

behavior_details.name: Burn HONEY
behavior_details.description: External User burns $HONEY to redeem collateral assets on Honey Swap.

data_flow_details.description: User sends collateral assets to Honey Swap to mint $HONEY.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: System state or logging does not immutably record which specific entitlement was fulfilled by a claim.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to deny a claim they have made, potentially leading to disputes or enabling double-claiming.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Could lead to conflicts over incentive ownership and potentially allow claiming the same incentive multiple times if state isn't properly managed.

assumptions: The Incentive Distributor Contract (ID 8) or associated state management does not immutably link a claim transaction to the specific incentive entitlement it paid out.
There is a way for a user to invalidate or disassociate from a past claim record.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure each claim transaction immutably consumes the specific entitlement it addresses and that this is recorded on-chain.

description: A user who has successfully claimed incentives might attempt to repudiate the claim, potentially exploiting flaws in the tracking mechanism of the Incentive Distributor Contract (ID 8) to attempt claiming the same incentives again or cause disputes.
